speaker: Interviewer
text: Dr. Aruna, you're trying to scale this IoT sensor network to 1,000+ farm clusters. That’s a massive deployment. What's currently keeping you up at night regarding the rural infrastructure?

speaker: Dr. Aruna P.
text: Connectivity is the brick wall. In most of these regions, 4G or 5G coverage is spotty at best. We have these high-quality soil sensors gathering data, but syncing that data to our central servers is a nightmare when the bandwidth is so low. We need a way to manage rural low-bandwidth sync reliably.

speaker: Interviewer
text: Are you processing data on the edge to reduce the payload?

speaker: Dr. Aruna P.
text: We're trying, but our current edge processing is quite basic. The real value is in our predictive yield modeling. If we can combine the local soil data with high-res satellite imagery, we can reach 95% yield accuracy. But that requires a lot of data merging that currently has to happen in the cloud.

speaker: Interviewer
text: How are the farmers reacting to these insights? Are they actually changing their irrigation or fertilizing habits based on your models?

speaker: Dr. Aruna P.
text: The trust is growing. When we can show them a 15% increase in crop yield because they followed our water management advice, they become our biggest advocates. The potential for these 'smart clusters' is enormous; we just need to solve the digital plumbing first.
